name: Selective Graph Coloring in Perfect Graphs

n.description: Number of vertices in the perfect graph

m.description: Number of edges in the perfect graph

E.description: Edges of the graph; each row lists the two endpoint vertices of an edge, with the smaller vertex index listed first

P.description: Number of disjoint clusters partitioning the vertex set

C.description: Binary cluster membership matrix: $C_{i,p}$ is 1 if vertex i belongs to cluster p, 0 otherwise

notes: Adapted from the Selective Graph Coloring (Sel-Col) problem in Seker, Ekim, and Taskin (2021), 'An exact cutting plane algorithm to solve the selective graph coloring problem in perfect graphs', European Journal of Operational Research 291.
We add implicit assumptions that $n$, $m$ are positive, that the clusters form a valid partition of the vertex set, and that every edge connects two distinct, valid vertex indices.
